reasoning: According to Article 21 of the German Basic Law, only the Federal Constitutional Court (BVerfG) has the authority to ban a political party. This process first requires a formal petition from the Bundestag, Bundesrat, or the Federal Government. Information up to May 2, 2025 indicates that while Parliament has debated drafting a petition, no final ban ruling is imminent. Crucially, even if a petition were fully submitted immediately, BVerfG proceedings for party bans are highly complex and take years to conclude. Historically, the ban of the SRP took 1.5 years, the KPD took 5 years, and the two failed attempts to ban the NPD took 2 and 3 years, respectively. Given that the resolution date is July 1, 2025—less than two months from the knowledge cutoff—it is procedurally and legally practically impossible for the BVerfG to complete its judicial process and officially announce a ban on the AfD in such a short timeframe. Therefore, I forecast the lowest allowed probability.
